title: Moderate Risk Portfolio Construction Rule
content:
This suitability rule governs the construction of moderate risk equity portfolios for clients of Meridian Wealth Partners, ensuring compliance with FINRA Rule 2111 suitability obligations and SEC Regulation BI best interest requirements. All associate-level and above personnel are authorized to implement these guidelines following proper client assessment procedures.

Client Profile Requirements: Moderate risk equity portfolios are appropriate for clients with investment time horizons of 5-15 years, moderate risk tolerance levels as determined by our standardized risk assessment questionnaire, and the financial capacity to withstand potential portfolio volatility of 12-18% annually. Clients must demonstrate liquid net worth exceeding $100,000 exclusive of primary residence, with investable assets representing no more than 70% of total net worth.

Asset Allocation Framework: Core equity allocation shall range between 60-80% of total portfolio value, with domestic large-cap holdings comprising 35-50% of equity exposure. International developed market exposure should constitute 15-25% of equity allocation, while emerging markets may represent 5-10% maximum. Fixed income allocation ranges from 20-35%, primarily consisting of investment-grade corporate bonds, Treasury securities, and high-quality municipal bonds with average duration between 3-7 years.

Equity Selection Criteria: Individual equity positions must maintain market capitalizations exceeding $2 billion at time of purchase, with no single holding representing more than 5% of total portfolio value. Sector concentration limits apply, restricting any individual sector to maximum 20% allocation except for technology and healthcare sectors, which may reach 25% given their market representation. All equity selections require minimum analyst coverage of three research firms and average daily trading volume exceeding $10 million.

Risk Management Parameters: Maximum portfolio beta relative to S&P 500 shall not exceed 1.15, while minimum diversification requires holdings across at least 8 distinct sectors. Trailing twelve-month portfolio volatility should remain within 10-20% range under normal market conditions. Monthly rebalancing triggers activate when asset class allocations drift beyond 5 percentage points from target weightings.

Performance Benchmarking: Moderate risk portfolios utilize a blended benchmark consisting of 70% MSCI ACWI Index and 30% Bloomberg Aggregate Bond Index. Performance attribution analysis occurs quarterly, examining sector allocation, security selection, and currency effects for international holdings. Risk-adjusted returns measured via Sharpe ratio should exceed benchmark by minimum 0.10 annually over rolling three-year periods.

Regulatory Compliance Considerations: In accordance with FINRA Rule 2111, comprehensive suitability analysis must document client investment profile, risk capacity, and alignment with stated financial objectives before portfolio implementation. SEC Regulation BI requirements mandate ongoing monitoring of portfolio alignment with client best interests, including fee impact analysis and alternative investment consideration. Documentation requirements include initial suitability determination, annual portfolio reviews, and material change assessments.

Rebalancing Protocols: Systematic rebalancing occurs quarterly unless tactical adjustments warrant interim modifications. Cash flows exceeding $25,000 trigger immediate allocation review to maintain target weightings. Tax-loss harvesting opportunities should be evaluated during rebalancing for taxable accounts, while retirement accounts prioritize strict adherence to strategic allocation targets.

This rule undergoes annual review by the Investment Committee and Compliance Department, with interim updates reflecting regulatory changes or market condition modifications. All portfolio construction activities require supervisory review for accounts exceeding $500,000 in value.
